title: ‘Hamlet’ premieres
subtitle: Play by William Shakespeare
fact: The Tragedy of Hamlet, Prince of Denmark, often shortened to Hamlet, is a tragedy written by William Shakespeare sometime between 1599 and 1601.

title: ‘Macbeth’ premieres
subtitle: Play by William Shakespeare
fact: The Tragedy of Macbeth, often shortened to Macbeth, is a tragedy by William Shakespeare, estimated to have been first performed in 1606.

title: ‘The Importance of Being Earnest’ premieres
subtitle: Play by Oscar Wilde
fact: The Importance of Being Earnest, a Trivial Comedy for Serious People is a play by Oscar Wilde, the last of his four drawing-room plays, following Lady Windermere's Fan (1892), A Woman of No Importance (1893) and An Ideal Husband (1895).

title: ‘A Midsummer Night's Dream’ premieres
subtitle: Play by William Shakespeare
fact: A Midsummer Night's Dream is a comedy play written by William Shakespeare in about 1595 or 1596.

title: ‘King Lear’ premieres
subtitle: Play by William Shakespeare
fact: The Tragedy of King Lear, often shortened to King Lear, is a tragedy written by William Shakespeare in late 1605 or early 1606.

title: ‘Othello’ premieres
subtitle: Play by William Shakespeare
fact: The Tragedy of Othello, the Moor of Venice, often shortened to Othello, is a tragedy written by William Shakespeare around 1603.

title: ‘The Tempest’ premieres
subtitle: Play by William Shakespeare
fact: The Tempest is a play by William Shakespeare, probably written in 1610–1611, and thought to be one of the last plays that he wrote alone.
